WORLDSCOPE/Sections/Ukrainian Internal/2026-05-29

Ukrainian Internal · 2026-05-29

Ukrainian press: national + Kyiv-local + government · 121 records across 11 sources

Kyiv Post · 83 records

Attack on Kyiv: What are Putin’s Objectives? — Russia’s latest missile and drone strikes on Kyiv, alongside warnings to foreign diplomats, are widely seen by commentators as signs of Kremlin weakness rather than strength. Analysts argue the

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

attacks reflect Putin’s nervousness, fading domestic support, and failed intimidation tactics, while exposing Europe’s slow response and Russia’s attempts to test advanced weapons and pressure Donald Trump.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Belarusian Components Found in Russian ‘Oreshnik’ Missile — Ukrainian officials showed European ambassadors foreign-made components extracted from Russian missiles and drones used in attacks against Ukraine, including parts from Belarusian

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

manufacturers found in the “Oreshnik” missile. Kyiv urged Europe to tighten sanctions enforcement against Russia and Belarus, warning that Moscow continues relying on foreign technologies and shadow fleet revenues to sustain its war effort.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Belarusian Democratic Forces Open Mission in Kyiv — On Tuesday, Belarusian democratic forces officially opened a mission in Kyiv aimed at strengthening cooperation with Ukraine and supporting Belarusians living in the country. The new offic

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

e will coordinate work with Ukrainian institutions, support volunteers and deepen political dialogue between Ukraine and Belarusian opposition groups amid threats of invasion from Belarus under the Lukashenko administration.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Brussels Finally Has a Timetable for Ukraine’s EU Bid — The European Commission is set to propose opening the first cluster of EU membership negotiations with Ukraine and Moldova on June 16, with EU leaders expected to approve the move days

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

later. Progress had long been blocked by Hungary’s Viktor Orbán, but his defeat in March’s election has cleared the way. New Prime Minister Péter Magyar has signaled a less obstructive stance, though he is tying cooperation to demands for frozen EU funds to b

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Chernihiv Hit by Massive Russian Attack, at Least 15 Explosions Reported — Chernihiv came under a massive Russian attack in the early hours of Wednesday, with around 15 explosions reported. Local officials said one city enterprise was damag

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ed, while no casualties had been reported.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Congress Finally Ready to Vote to Help Ukraine, Punish Russia — Congress has the power to act now to help Ukraine and punish Russia when the Trump Administration will not. The US House of Representatives is finally ready to vote the first w

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

eek in June on Russian sanctions legislation to help Ukraine and punish Russia for its illegal aggression and ongoing war crimes against the people of Ukraine.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Dragon Capital Closes Ukraine's First Infrastructure Fund at $240 Million; EBRD, IFC, and EIB Anchor the Round — Dragon Capital and Amber Infrastructure have completed the first close of the Amber Dragon Ukraine Infrastructure Fund I, raisi

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ng €207 million ($240 million) from anchor investors including the EBRD, EIB, and IFC. The fund will target energy, transport, and digital sectors, with first deals expected imminently. The company called it the first dedicated infrastructure vehicle in Ukrain

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Drones Strike 3 Russian ‘Shadow Fleet’ Tankers in Black Sea Near Turkey — Three Russia-linked “shadow fleet” tankers were reportedly attacked by drones in the Black Sea near Turkey, with no casualties. Ukraine’s HUR says the vessels were us

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ed to evade sanctions.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

EU Approves €2.8B Ukraine Facility Payment — The EU Council on May 28 approved a €2.8 billion ($3.25 billion) disbursement to Ukraine under the Ukraine Facility after Kyiv completed 11 of 20 required reform steps. Ukraine has now fulfilled

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

86 reform benchmarks in total, with another 65 underway, and will for the first time receive compensation for reforms completed ahead of schedule.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Europe Cannot Be Both Ukraine’s Ally and Russia’s Negotiating Partner – Norway’s Foreign Minister — Norwegian Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide said Europe should not act as a mediator in Russia-Ukraine peace talks and should instead increa

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

se military support for Ukraine. Estonian and EU officials echoed this view, arguing neutrality would weaken Europe’s leverage and could ultimately advantage the Kremlin

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Europe Warns Russia Could Escalate War Beyond Ukraine — European security officials on Tuesday reported concerns about Russia potentially escalating the war beyond Ukraine to justify another mobilization wave and pressure NATO. The fears co

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

me amid Russian nuclear drills in Belarus, threats toward Baltic states and mounting Russian battlefield losses.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Explainer: Russia’s Grip on Its Neighbors Is Loosening — Russia’s war on Ukraine has reshaped Moscow’s relationships across its former sphere of influence. Kazakhstan and Azerbaijan have quietly distanced themselves while preserving pragmat

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ic ties with Russia. Armenia has made the most dramatic pivot, freezing CSTO membership and deepening EU and US partnerships. Moldova is pushing ahead with EU integration and Transnistria reintegration. Georgia remains an outlier, with its government drifting

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

EXPLAINER: So Where Does Ukraine Get So Many ‘Ace’ Drone Pilots From? — Ukraine’s “drone advantage” isn’t just airframes, it’s pilot training. Ukraine runs a massive drone program with ~80,000 personnel involved and an estimated 25-40,000 a

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ctive combat UAV pilots (elite SBS: ~15k) – outnumbering many nations’ entire air forces. Training grew from 2,014 hobbyists to a nationwide network of military, private, and unit-run schools, turning novices into competent pilots in about one to two months. H

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Hungary Lawmakers Vote to Stop Orban-Initiated ICC Exit — Hungary’s parliament voted to halt the country’s withdrawal from the International Criminal Court, reversing a move launched by former Prime Minister Viktor Orbán. Pro-EU leader Pete

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

r Magyar pushed the repeal through parliament ahead of the June 2 deadline, signaling renewed support for the Hague-based tribunal.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Hungary Links Zelensky Meeting to Progress on Minority Rights — Hungary’s government on Wednesday said a potential meeting between Prime Minister Péter Magyar and President Volodymyr Zelensky will only take place if negotiations over the ri

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ghts of the Hungarian minority in Ukraine make progress. Technical-level talks between Budapest and Kyiv are already underway as both sides seek to ease diplomatic tensions and improve bilateral relations.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

IMF Mission Arrives in Kyiv as Key Reforms Stall — An IMF mission led by Gavin Gray arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday to begin the first review of Ukraine’s $8.1 billion Extended Fund Facility program, with a $685.5 million tranche expected in e

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

arly June. Reform progress is mixed: parliament approved the military levy extension, but failed to pass VAT on small parcels, while a proposal on VAT for sole proprietors remains under discussion.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent
country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kallas Says Limits Demanded From Ukraine Must Be ‘Mirrored’ to Russia — EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Russia should face the same military restrictions it demands from Ukraine in any future peace agreement. Speaking on Thursday,

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Kallas argued that Moscow’s conditions remain “maximalist” and said European security concerns also include Russian troops stationed in countries such as Georgia and Moldova.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kharkiv Opens First Underground Kindergarten Amid Intensifying Russian Strikes — Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov says the city has been compelled to build 10 underground schools and is currently constructing its first underground kindergarten.

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Speaking at the GLOBSEC Forum, he said Russian attacks on civilian infrastructure have reshaped children’s education in Kharkiv, and called on the international community to continue supporting Ukraine.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kremlin Deepens Taliban Partnership With New Military-Technical Agreement — Russia and the Taliban signed a military-technical cooperation agreement during a security forum in the Moscow region, highlighting Moscow’s steadily expanding ties

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

with Kabul. The contents of the agreement have not been officially disclosed. The move follows Russia’s suspension of the Taliban’s terrorist designation in 2025.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Kyiv Briefly Under Air Alert Over Ballistic Missile Threat — Kyiv and several Ukrainian regions were briefly placed under air raid alerts in the early hours of Friday after the Air Force warned of a ballistic missile threat to eastern, nort

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

hern and central regions. The warning came amid repeated Russian missile threats, including from MiG-31K aircraft capable of carrying Kinzhal ballistic missiles.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Latvia Gets New Centre-Right Govt After Row Over Stray Ukraine Drones — Latvia’s parliament approved a new centre-right coalition government led by Andris Kulbergs after the previous cabinet collapsed over a dispute involving stray Ukrainia

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

n drones and air defence failures. The new government pledged stronger security, continued support for Ukraine, and a firm pro-EU, pro-NATO foreign policy ahead of October elections.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Latvia Installs ‘Dragon Teeth’ on Russian Border as NATO Assigns New Corps to Defend Baltics — Latvia has begun installing concrete “dragon’s teeth” anti-tank barriers on its border with Russia, as a part of the Baltic Defense Line agreed b

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

y Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania. At the same time, NATO is preparing to assign the German-Netherlands Corps, based in Münster, splitting responsibility on the eastern flank with the existing Multinational Corps Northeast in Szczecin.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

More Than 50 Countries Condemn Russia’s Mass Attack on Ukraine at UN — A global coalition of more than 50 countries backed Ukraine at the UN after Russia’s latest mass attack, condemning missile strikes, embassy threats and nuclear-capable

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

weapons.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

NATO Plans Rapid Troop Deployment For Baltic Defence In Case Of War With Russia — NATO, as of Tuesday, is reportedly preparing plans to rapidly deploy additional corps-level forces to defend the Baltic states in case of conflict with Russia

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

, according to Reuters. The move follows Moscow expanding legal provisions for overseas troop use and renewed rhetoric over the rights of Russians in Estonia, Latvia and Lithuania.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Northolt Treaty Deepens UK-Poland Defense Ties Amid Fears for NATO’s Future — UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk have signed the Northolt Treaty, a defense and security pact that will strengthen air def

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ense cooperation and joint efforts against Russian hybrid threats, as well as joint action plan on irregular migration. The deal comes amid reports of Russia stepping up troop mobilization, with recent drone and missile incidents concerning NATO’s eastern flan

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Norway Contributes €40 Million to Ukraine’s Winter Energy Resilience — Norway will provide €40 million ($46 million) through the EU’s Ukraine Investment Framework to help Ukraine strengthen its energy system before winter, focusing on decen

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

tralized power, renewables, battery storage and local resilience amid continued Russian attacks.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Poland’s Orlen Energy Group Says It Is ‘in Talks’ Over a Stake in Ukraine’s Ukrnafta — Poland’s largest energy concern Orlen has entered talks over acquiring a stake in Ukrnafta, CEO Ireneusz Fafara said Thursday, describing the company as

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

one of several partners in an active negotiation process. The move follows years of deepening commercial ties: Orlen’s Mažeikiai refinery sells nearly 18 percent of its output in Ukraine, and the group ships around 1.5 million tons of fuel products there annua

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Putin Travels Through Kazakhstan Under Heavy Security — Russian leader Vladimir Putin on Thursday traveled through Kazakhstan’s capital under heavy security measures that included armored vehicles, motorcycles, military personnel and aerial

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

surveillance, according to footage.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Putin, Tokayev Sign ‘7 Principles of Friendship’ — Russian President Vladimir Putin and Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signed a joint declaration outlining seven principles of friendship and cooperation between Russia and Kazakhstan

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

during Putin’s state visit to Astana. The document emphasizes shared history, economic partnership, Eurasian integration and cultural ties.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Rada Ratifies Zelensky’s €90B EU Loan for Ukraine’s Defense and Budget Support — The Verkhovna Rada has ratified a bill submitted by President Zelensky approving an EU loan package of up to €90 billion ($104 billion) for Ukraine. The fundin

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

g, planned for 2026–2027, will support both defense spending and state budget stability. The agreement includes concessional terms, with interest covered by the EU and repayment of principal linked to potential reparations from Russia.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Resilience Within Music — After Russia’s full-scale invasion scattered her choir across Europe, a pianist in Kharkiv rebuilt a community where singing together offers a brief distance from the war surrounding the city.

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent
country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Rubio’s Yerevan Visit: What Washington Said, and What It Left Unspoken — US Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s brief stop in Yerevan signaled support for Armenia’s sovereignty without openly endorsing any candidate before the election. By foc

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

using on concrete agreements, Washington denied Moscow an easy propaganda weapon.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russia Deploys New Air Defense Systems on Moscow Rooftops — Russia is reinforcing Moscow’s air defenses by installing Pantsir-SMD-E systems on rooftops across the city, including civilian buildings and business centers, according to analyst

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

s and media reports published Thursday.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Hits Apartment Building in NATO Member Romania, Injuring Two — Romania’s defense ministry said a Russian drone entered Romanian airspace during Moscow’s overnight attacks on Ukraine and crashed onto the roof of an apartment bu

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ilding in Galați. The impact triggered a fire, and emergency services said two people were wounded.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Strike Hits Cargo Ship En Route From Odesa Region To Turkey, Ukraine Says — A cargo ship ANT sailing from Ukraine’s Odesa region to Turkey was struck Friday morning by a drone in the Black Sea, causing a fire and injuring two

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

crew members, Ukraine’s Navy said. The vessel was later evacuated and the blaze extinguished. Kyiv attributed the attack to Russian forces, warning of continued risks to civilian shipping.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russian Drone Strike Injures 11 in Odesa Region — Russian forces launched a drone attack against civilian infrastructure in Ukraine’s Odesa region late Wednesday, injuring 11 people, including two children, according to regional authorities

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

. Residential buildings, a Nova Poshta branch and a grocery store were damaged in the strike. Three victims remain in serious condition as emergency crews continue clearing the aftermath of the attack.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Russia’s War Costs Could Exceed Budget by $28 Billion — Russia’s war spending could surpass planned budget levels by 2 trillion rubles ($28 billion) this year, according to a report citing a letter from Finance Minister Anton Siluanov. The

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

document reportedly warned of growing fiscal pressures and urged spending freezes in other sectors to offset rising military costs. The report highlights mounting strains on Russia’s finances due to record defense spending.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Sweden Hands Ukraine 16 Gripen Jets Armed With 200-km Missiles, Opens Deal for 22 More — Sweden announced it will transfer 16 Gripen C/D fighter jets to Ukraine free of charge and plans to sell 22 additional Gripen E aircraft. The agreement

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

was unveiled during a joint briefing by Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson and President Volodymyr Zelensky at Uppsala Air Base. Kyiv says the jets, equipped with long-range missiles, will help push Russian aircraft farther from Ukrainian territory and strengthen

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Sybiha Calls for Airport or Seaport Truce as First Step Toward Peace — Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha said Europe should first push for a limited airport or seaport truce, using sanctions and frozen Russian assets to press Moscow toward pra

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ctical peace steps.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Thousands Rally in Georgia for Europe on Independence Day — Thousands of Georgians rallied in Tbilisi on Independence Day, demanding a European future and protesting the government’s democratic backsliding.

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent
country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Trump Endorses Pashinyan Ahead of Crucial Armenia Vote — US President Donald Trump has endorsed Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan ahead of a key parliamentary election, praising his peace agenda and plans for the US-backed “Trump Rout

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

e” corridor. The endorsement came a day after Secretary of State Marco Rubio visited Yerevan, where Washington and Armenia signed agreements on strategic partnership, critical minerals and regional connectivity.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Trump Threatened or Attacked 1 in 13 Countries, CNN Says — US President Donald Trump has threatened or used force against at least 15 countries during his time in office, according to a CNN analysis published on Wednesday, after Trump warne

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

d Oman it could be “blown up” over tensions in the Strait of Hormuz.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Trump’s Gaza Peace Board Raises Zero Dollars, Reports Say — Trump’s Board of Peace for Gaza has raised no money into its official World Bank fund four months after launch and multibillion-dollar pledges, the Financial Times reported. Instea

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

d, donors have sent limited contributions to a private JPMorgan account that is not subject to the same independent oversight, with none of the promised US support being deployed for rebuilding on the ground yet.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Allocates $244M for Weapons Production, Defense Expansion — The Ukrainian government approved an additional Hr.10.8 billion ($244 million) in defense funding for weapons procurement and domestic arms production, Most of the funds wi

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ll go toward purchasing, repairing and modernizing military equipment, while nearly Hr.2 billion will support expansion of Ukraine’s defense industry and battlespace technologies.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Bets on Deep Strikes With ‘Logistical Lockdown’ to Drain Russian Offensive Power — Ukraine’s Ministry of Defense, together with the General Staff, has launched a new program called “Logistical Lockdown” to expand deep strikes on Rus

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

sian rear infrastructure and logistics networks. Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov said the initiative aims to systematically degrade Russian operational capacity and reduce frontline assaults. Kyiv also reports rising Russian losses per kilometer of advance,

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Claims Crimea–Donbas Land Corridor Effectively Cut As Logistics Routes Come Under Fire — Ukraine’s military intelligence (HUR) on Friday said it is disrupting Russian logistics in occupied southern Ukraine by targeting the land corr

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

idor linking Crimea with Donbas. Officials claim key routes between Berdyansk, Melitopol, and Dzhankoi are under fire control, with strikes destroying fuel and transport vehicles and worsening Russia’s supply and infrastructure capacity in the occupied territo

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Fortifies Belarus Border Amid Growing Threats — Ukraine is strengthening its entire border with Belarus, with new engineering works along the frontier and deeper fortifications behind it. Officials say the whole 1,085-kilometer line

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

is already protected, but construction is expanding as Kyiv prepares for possible Russian escalation from the north.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Has 500 Targets Ready in Belarus if Lukashenko Joins War, ‘Magyar’ Says — Ukraine says it has already marked 500 targets in Belarus if Lukashenko enters the war, as tensions rise over alleged drone attacks near the border.

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent
country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Hits Russian Aircraft Plant, Airbase and Oil Refinery in Overnight Barrage — Ukraine launched overnight strikes on Russian military and energy infrastructure, reportedly hitting an aircraft repair plant in Taganrog, the Baltimor air

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

base in Voronezh, and the Tuapse oil refinery. Occupied Sevastopol also came under missile and drone attack as Moscow claimed to down 140 Ukrainian drones.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Issues Nationwide Missile Warning Over MiG-31K Takeoff — Ukraine issued a nationwide missile threat warning early Friday after detecting the takeoff of a Russian MiG-31K, a carrier of the Kinzhal ballistic missile. Air raid alerts w

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ere activated across all regions, and residents were urged to take shelter.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Launches First War-Risk Insurance Payouts as 4 Kyiv Firms Cut Premium Costs by Two-Thirds — Ukraine’s Export Credit Agency has issued its first payouts under a state war-risk insurance subsidy program, with four Kyiv businesses shar

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ing Hr. 6.8 million ($153,500) in compensation. The program, established under Cabinet Resolution No. 1541, reduces effective insurance costs from 4.24% to 1.19%. The ECA has received 40 applications from companies across multiple regions, covering assets wort

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Names Bosnian-Serb Fighter a Suspect in War Crime Probe — Ukraine said Davor Savičić, a Bosnian-Serb mercenary fighting for Moscow, has been named a suspect for reportedly torturing civilians north of Kyiv in 2022. Other reports say

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

Savičić previously fought for Serbian paramilitary groups in the Yugoslav civil war and later helped Russia recruit other mercenaries.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Offers Romania Drone Defense Cooperation After Russian Drone Hits Apartment Block — Ukraine is ready to deepen cooperation with Romania to counter Russian drone threats after a Russian drone struck a residential building in Galați o

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

n Friday. Foreign Minister Andriy Sybiha said the incident highlights the broader risk Russia’s war poses to the Black Sea region and Europe, while NATO and EU officials condemned it as a dangerous escalation.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Ready to Buy Patriots as Zelensky Warns Russia Prepares New Wave of ‘Terror Strikes’ — President Zelensky appealed to US President Trump and Congress for expanded air defense support, warning that Russia is escalating missile and dr

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

one attacks on Ukraine. He said a recent strike included hundreds of drones and dozens of missiles and stressed that Ukraine remains heavily dependent on US Patriot systems to counter ballistic threats. Zelensky also called for deeper defense cooperation and w

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine Ready to Pay for More Patriot Systems, Stefanishyna Says — Kyiv is prepared to pay for additional Patriot air defense systems and missiles as Russia escalates attacks against Ukrainian cities, Ukraine’s Ambassador to the US Olha Ste

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

fanishyna said on Thursday after Zelensky sent an urgent letter to Trump and the US Congress warning of critical shortages. Kyiv says ballistic missile defense remains almost entirely dependent on the US.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine to Be Fully Integrated Into EU Air Defense, Drone Priorities, Von der Leyen Says — European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said Ukraine will be included in Europe’s urgent defense efforts, backed by a €28.3 billion ($32.9

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

billion) loan for Kyiv’s military needs.

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

Ukraine's Top Mobile Operator Bets $81M on Sun to Cut Grid Dependence — Kyivstar has acquired six solar power plants in the Lviv region for Hr.3.6 billion ($80.8 million), lifting its total renewable energy portfolio to 118 MW, enough to co

Kyiv Postmainstream_independent

ver roughly 30% of the company’s annual electricity consumption. The deal, cleared by Ukraine’s Antimonopoly Committee in March 2026, is the latest step in a broader energy and digital expansion strategy the Nasdaq-listed telecoms operator has been executing s

country-ukrainenewsroom-kyiv-post

віабомб – Генштаб) — За добу 28 травня Генеральний штаб зафіксував 267 бойових зіткнень на фронті.

[TITLE: Міноборони Румунії назвало тип дрона РФ, що вибухнув у будинку в Галаці | LEDE: Міністерство національної оборони Румунії заявило в п'ятницю вранці, що безпілотник, який врізався у житловий буд] (uk: Міноборони Румунії назвало тип д

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

рона РФ, що вибухнув у будинку в Галаці) — Міністерство національної оборони Румунії заявило в п'ятницю вранці, що безпілотник, який врізався у житловий будинок у Галаці та вибухнув, був російським дроном типу "Герань-2".

country-ukrainenewsroom-ukrainska-pravda

[TITLE: Німеччина не проти відмовитися від назви "асоційоване членство" щодо шляху України до ЄС | LEDE: Офіційний Берлін не проти не проти відмовитися від назви "асоційоване членство" для ідеї канцлер] (uk: Німеччина не проти відмовитися в

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

ід назви "асоційоване членство" щодо шляху Украї) — Офіційний Берлін не проти не проти відмовитися від назви "асоційоване членство" для ідеї канцлера Фрідріха Мерца щодо шляху України до ЄС, яку критикують як невдалу.

зділ ССО назвали на честь Героїв УПА) — У Польщі викликало обурення рішення Володимира Зеленського присвоїти найменування на честь Героїв УПА елітному підрозділу Сил спеціальних операцій.

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

ден у Зеленського, бо той присвоїв підрозділу СС) — Польський президент Кароль Навроцький буде домагатися позбавлення президента України Володимира Зеленського найвищої нагороди Польщі через його рішення присвоїти елітному підрозділу українських ССО імені Геро

[TITLE: Президент Румунії скликав Раду нацбезпеки через влучання дрона у Галаці, в МЗС викликали посла РФ | LEDE: Очільниця МЗС Румунії Оана Цою повідомила в п'ятницю, що викликала посла Російської Фед] (uk: Президент Румунії скликав Раду н

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

ацбезпеки через влучання дрона у Галаці, в МЗС в) — Очільниця МЗС Румунії Оана Цою повідомила в п'ятницю, що викликала посла Російської Федерації, щоб повідомити йому про заходи, яких вживатиме Румунія на дипломатичному рівні після того, як російський безпілот

country-ukrainenewsroom-ukrainska-pravda

[TITLE: Росія атакувала Україну "Іскандером" та 323 БпЛА, 217 дронів знешкодили | LEDE: Російські війська вночі атакували Україну ракетою і 232 дронами, ППО знешкодила 217 дронів.] (uk: Росія атакувала Україну "Іскандером" та 323 БпЛА, 217

дронів знешкодили) — Російські війська вночі атакували Україну ракетою і 232 дронами, ППО знешкодила 217 дронів.

[TITLE: Свириденко: Законопроєкт про реформу ДБР планують внести до Ради у грудні | LEDE: Прем'єр-міністерка Юлія Свириденко каже, що законопроєкт про реформу Державного бюро розслідувань мають напрацю] (uk: Свириденко: Законопроєкт про реф

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

орму ДБР планують внести до Ради у грудні) — Прем'єр-міністерка Юлія Свириденко каже, що законопроєкт про реформу Державного бюро розслідувань мають напрацювати до кінця року, а внести до Верховної Ради планують у грудні 2026 року.

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

докремленні Молдови від України на шляху до ЄС) — Посол Німеччини в Україні Гайко Томс каже, що Берлін не бачить ризиків у потенційному відокремленні пари Україна-Молдова на шляху до ЄС у разі реалізації ідеї канцлера Фрідріха Мерца про спецстатус для України.

'єкти для зберігання пально) — У Росії заявили про атаку безпілотників і перекрили трасу з Ярославля на Москву.

[TITLE: У Румунії пояснили, чому не збили російський дрон, який врізався в житловий будинок | LEDE: Причиною того, що у румунському місті Галац не збили російський дрон, стали брак часу та обмеження, в] (uk: У Румунії пояснили, чому не збил

Ukrainska Pravdamainstream_independent

и російський дрон, який врізався в житловий буди) — Причиною того, що у румунському місті Галац не збили російський дрон, стали брак часу та обмеження, в умовах яких змушена працювати армія країни, заявив представник Міноброни Румунії.

Румунії, у повіті Марамуреш, було виявлено дрон без вибухового навантаження.

роном у Румунії Росія перетнула ще одну межу) — Глава Єврокомісії Урсула фон дер Ляєн сказала, що інцидент із падінням російського дрона на житловий будинок у Румунії демонструє перетин Росією ще однієї межі.

риводом застосування 4 статті НАТО – глава МЗС Р) — Міністерка закордонних справ Румунії Оана Цою заявила, що падіння російського дрона на житловий будинок у Галаці є приводом для застосування статті 4 НАТО щодо консультацій з союзниками.

д міністерством перетворять на сквер) — <p>У Варшаві парковку на площі Трьох Хрестів перетворять на сквер. Це не перше озеленення площі, три роки тому там висадили 28 дорослих дерев, зокрема липи та груші. Дерева тоді допомагали висаджувати діти з місцевих сад

перше встановили кондиціонери (фото)) — <p>У регіональних електропоїздах вперше встановлено кондиціонери, повідомляє пресслужба Укрзалізниці. Кондиціонерами обладнали дві модернізовані електрички на маршрутах Рівне–Львів і Київ–Хмельницький. Електропоїзди ЕР9М

Hmarochosmainstream_independent

вництво оформлюватиме ШІ) — <p>Офіс із видачі дозволів у Денвері, штат Колорадо, почав використовувати ШІ, щоб прискорити видачу документів. Місто сподівається, що це допоможе подолати черги, у яких люди подекуди стоять понад пів року. Для цього обрали програм

country-ukrainecity-kyivnewsroom-hmarochos

Hmarochosmainstream_independent

у облігаціями (подробиці)) — <p>Верховна Рада планує залучити додаткові кошти на іпотеку спеціальними облігаціями. Фінансовий комітет парламенту вже підтримав відповідний законопроєкт №15172, автором якого став Данило Гетманцев. Закон має допомогти банкам знай

ю ставав все яскравішим і зараз — вогняно-червон) — <p>Фасад ліцею №8 на вулиці Вишгородській, 6, що на Пріорці в Оболонському районі Києва нещодавно пофарбували у яскраві червоні та помаранчеві кольори. Школу було засновано 1937 року. Її збудували за типовим

аршрутки, що не відповідають вимогам? Пояснює Де) — <p>Якщо на конкурс на той чи інший маршрут Києва не подаються перевізники з автобусами, що повністю відповідають вимогам, умови послаблюють. Про це йдеться у відповіді Департаменту транспортної інфраструктури
